preamble: The shift from the postalveolar affricate [tʃ] to the interdental fricative [θ] is a rare two-step process involving deaffrication (loss of the stop component) followed by extreme fronting. It is documented as a highly specific regional feature within the broader Spanish 'ceceo' dialect continuum.

examples.note: Reported in extreme ceceante rural varieties of Western Andalusia; standard ceceo affects sibilants /s/, not typically [tʃ]. This extension to [tʃ] is marginal and poorly documented.
